key_insight: MCH-Guard is a multimodal machine-learning framework that stratifies cerebral microhemorrhage risk in Alzheimer’s disease using clinical, fluid, and imaging data, with strong performance even in a clinical-only model.
clinical_relevance: It could support safer anti-amyloid therapy monitoring by identifying patients at higher risk for microhemorrhage and ARIA-H, including in settings with limited biomarker access.
method: Trained nested predictive models on ADNI data (N=813).
Integrated demographics/clinical history, fluid biomarkers, and imaging to predict baseline MCH, incidence, and stability.
Built longitudinal models for time-to-onset and four-year risk stratification, including a transient vascular instability phenotype.
evidence_signal: In the abstract, the comprehensive model achieved AUC 0.86 for baseline MCH detection, while the minimal clinical-history model still performed well at AUC 0.82 and the time-to-onset model reached R2=0.68.
watch_item: Performance was evaluated in ADNI and may need external validation before use in other populations or clinical workflows.
paper_title: MCH-Guard: Multimodal Machine Learning Framework for Risk Stratification of Cerebral Microhemorrhage Risk in the Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative
